title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
summary: Tara Agyemang presents WebMCP, a proposed web standard that enables AI agents to interact with web applications through structured tools instead of screen scraping. The standard offers both declarative HTML attributes for simple forms and imperative JavaScript APIs for complex workflows, with early preview in Chrome 146.
writeup: Tara Agyemang of Google presents WebMCP, a proposed web standard that lets AI agents act on web applications through structured tools rather than screen scraping. The standard exposes two interfaces: declarative HTML attributes for simple forms and imperative JavaScript APIs for complex workflows. WebMCP matters because it shifts agent interaction from brittle DOM parsing to defined tool calling, giving sites control over the actions agents can invoke. An early preview ships in Chrome 146.
